name: Cheyenne-Laramie County Employees
description_short: Cheyenne-Laramie County Employees FCU is a member-owned federal credit union serving local government employees with savings accounts, loans, and digital banking services.
description_long:
Cheyenne-Laramie County Employees Federal Credit Union is a not-for-profit, member-owned financial institution based in Cheyenne, Wyoming. The credit union serves employees of Cheyenne and Laramie County government entities, offering NCUA-insured deposit accounts and lending products at competitive rates. The organization maintains a physical branch location at 4523 Driftwood Drive in Cheyenne and operates under routing number 307086604.

The credit union provides a comprehensive range of financial services including share savings accounts, share certificates, personal loans, credit cards, and specialized lending products like gap insurance and NADA blue book loan quotes. Members can access funds through drive-thru service, after-hours depositories, ATM networks, and online banking via their E-Teller platform. The institution also offers insurance products including AD&D coverage (up to $1,000 free), credit life insurance, and disability insurance. Digital payment options include Apple Pay, Samsung Pay, and Google Pay integration with their VISA debit and credit cards.

The credit union distinguishes itself through member-specific benefits including free notary service for members only, payroll deduction loan options, and overdraft protection services. They emphasize fast loan approval with reasonable rates and maintain partnerships such as Centennial Lending for specialized borrowing needs. The organization also provides wire transfer services through their correspondent bank, Alloya Corporate FCU, and maintains active fraud prevention education for members.

As a credit union, membership eligibility is restricted to Cheyenne-Laramie County government employees, which limits the potential membership base compared to community banks. The website provides limited transparency on specific loan rates, APRs, or detailed fee schedules beyond a referenced fee schedule link. The institution's digital presence and online application capabilities suggest adequate technology infrastructure, though marketing materials focus primarily on local service delivery rather than competitive positioning against regional or national alternatives.
services: VISA credit cards with digital payment integration
VISA debit cards with card blocking capability
Gap insurance and NADA blue book loan quotes
pros: Free notary service exclusively available to members
AD&D insurance coverage up to $1,000 provided at no cost
Multiple digital payment options (Apple Pay, Samsung Pay, Google Pay) integrated with VISA cards
Drive-thru and after-hours depository services for convenient access
Overdraft protection and fast loan approval with stated reasonable rates
Direct deposit and payroll deduction options for members
Wire transfer services available through correspondent banking
Online 24/7 application access for member convenience
cons: Membership restricted to Cheyenne-Laramie County government employees only, limiting eligibility
Website does not disclose specific loan rates, APRs, or detailed fee information publicly
Limited branch presence with single physical location in Cheyenne
No mention of mobile banking app despite digital payment capabilities
Minimal information about credit card rewards, benefits, or terms
best_for: Cheyenne and Laramie County government employees seeking member-owned institution benefits
Local residents wanting free notary services and payroll-linked banking solutions
Members preferring drive-thru and after-hours access to banking services
diagnosis: Best for eligible Cheyenne-Laramie County government employees seeking a not-for-profit alternative with free member services and reasonable loan rates. Primary limitation is restricted membership eligibility; only government employees in the service area can join, making it unavailable to the general public.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Cheyenne-Laramie County Employees — NCUA-insured credit union in Cheyenne, WY. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
